title: React Portals Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of createPortal, use cases like modals, and event bubbling in portals.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary purpose of a React Portal?
options: To render components in a different thread.
To render children into a DOM node that exists outside the parent component's DOM hierarchy.
To speed up the app by caching components.
To create a global state.

questions.question: Which function should you use to create a portal in React?
options: createPortal()
newPortal()
usePortal()
makePortal()

questions.question: What is the result when an event is fired inside a portal (even if the portal's DOM element is outside the parent)?
options: The event does not bubble up.
The event bubbles up the portal's actual DOM tree.
The event bubbles up the React component tree to its parent/ancestors.
The event is automatically cancelled.

questions.question: For which of the following use cases is a React Portal commonly used?
options: Modals
Tooltips
Global alerts
All of the above

questions.question: Does createPortal() need a DOM element as its second argument?
options: Yes, such as document.body or an element selected by ID.
No, it can determine where to render automatically.
Yes, but only in functional components.
Only if you use class-based syntax.
